accepted_enrichment_summary.acceptance_policy: A PubMed article candidate is materialized only when the acceptance audit marks it as machine_acceptance_candidate_cross_source: review-ready high anchor, confidence >= 0.95, at least four non-name anchors, and an NPI secondary identity anchor for the same person_key.

attending_trend_acceptance_summary.acceptance_policy: No recent-attending trend candidate is accepted as a trend fact until explicit reviewer acceptance confirms same-person identity, current Penn endpoint, training line, program type, and dates.
attending_trend_acceptance_summary.required_reviewer_action: Confirm same-person identity, current Penn endpoint, training line, program type, and dates before promoting to an accepted trend fact.

attending_trend_review_claims_summary.acceptance_policy: Materialize only current Penn attending groups with a Penn-training profile claim and a dated recent official Penn Faculty Biosketch residency/fellowship bridge. Keep as review-ready until an explicit reviewer acceptance decision is recorded.
attending_trend_review_claims_summary.required_reviewer_action: Confirm same-person identity, current Penn endpoint, training line, program type, and dates before promoting to an accepted trend fact.

contact_assurance_summary.acceptance_policy: No public contact candidate is treated as a verified contact fact until a current official source or directory reconfirms contact value, domain, person identity, and intended scope.

enrichment_acceptance_summary.acceptance_rule: Do not mutate roster truth. A PubMed article becomes a machine-acceptance candidate only with review_ready_high_anchor, confidence >= 0.95, at least four non-name anchors, and an NPI secondary identity anchor for the same person.

official_gap_roster_program_resolution_s.mutation_policy: non_mutating_audit; denominator_mutation_allowed marks reviewer-ready candidates but this script does not close coverage gaps

official_program_alias_review_packets_su.mutation_policy: non_mutating_alias_review_packets; explicit reviewer acceptance is required before denominator coverage mutation

top_packets.official_program_name: Pulmonary Disease and Critical Care Medicine

official_program_coverage_action_queue_s.mutation_policy: non_mutating_action_queue; actions require source-backed parser output or explicit reviewer acceptance before coverage truth changes

top_actions.official_program_name: Pulmonary Disease and Critical Care Medicine

official_program_coverage_assurance_summ.mutation_policy: non_mutating_assurance_audit; coverage_mutation_allowed is reserved for future explicit reviewer acceptance

program_identifier_candidate_summary.acceptance_rule: Use ACGME code as a program identifier candidate only; do not mutate trainee roster truth or lifecycle rules without review when local tracks, duplicate sites, or non-ACGME programs are possible.

program_identifier_reconciliation_summar.acceptance_rule: Accept only a single strong top ACGME candidate with no close competing Penn/UPHS facility, affiliate, or track sibling.

program_lifecycle_consistency_summary.acceptance_rule: Use accepted ACGME identifiers for lifecycle rollups only when official coverage mapping and a single non-default lifecycle code agree.

top_rows.utility_label: Training state machine and longitudinal readiness
Official Penn faculty biosketch training bridges

training_state_transition_plan_summary.policy: This ledger is non-mutating. Expected transitions are classifiable only with fresh source evidence or explicit terminal absence after stale-after; review/source-bound states are not carried forward silently.

warehouse_reproducibility_summary.acceptance_rule: A warehouse is reproducible only when required artifacts exist and row counts match their SQLite tables; binary size warnings do not invalidate data, but they require repository storage action.
